mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0% within a single month, it signals a labor market inflection that historically precedes a recessionary earnings drag, depressing S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer credit balances indicates a supply-demand mismatch where inventory drawdowns outpace spending, leading to margin compression and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated policy rates, a contraction in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for broader demand weakness, depressing equity valuations over the subsequent month as construction-linked earnings fade.
failure_condition: HOUST declines do not correlate with negative SPX forward returns in out-of-sample testing, or the predictive R-squared remains below 0.02 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reinforces corporate revenue visibility, supporting multiple expansion and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive SPX returns do not materialize in the majority of months with payroll growth above 100k, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises above 255.0, it forces markets to price in prolonged restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 50% of instances where CPI crosses the threshold, or the signal exhibits no statistical edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production accelerates while consumer credit growth stagnates signals a buildup of unsold inventory, foreshadowing corporate earnings downgrades and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction between INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict negative returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample windows, or the cross-product term in a predictive regression is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary cycle, a sudden spike in the unemployment rate acts as a recession warning that triggers risk-off positioning, causing sharp equity drawdowns over the next week as discount rates and earnings expectations move inversely.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels (partial correlation < 0.05).
